role_summary: Civil rights movement participant; co-subject of a joint oral history with her husband David Mercer Ackerman (catalog entry 135).
ai_reading: Satoko Ito Ackerman shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 135 (catalog slug 'david-mercer-ackerman-and-satoko-ito-ackerman'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Satoko Ito Ackerman's Civil Rights History Project oral history is cross-referenced against the Library of Congress published transcript. Satoko Ito Ackerman is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 135 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Satoko Ito Ackerman; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 135
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: Civil rights marchers carrying American flags on the road from Selma to Montgomery, Alabama, in 1965
gallery.caption: Marchers on the 1965 Selma to Montgomery march, which Ackerman recalls traveling to and joining after a classmate at Chicago Theological Seminary urged students to take part.
